content.question: What is the precise marginal contribution of six- and eight-year selective tracks to socioeconomic and Roma segregation compared to the broader free-school-choice and catchment-school system?
content.reduction_path: Commission a decomposition analysis using national enrollment and family-background data (preferably linked to administrative sources on selective-track admission, catchment-school assignment, and independent-school choice) to isolate the segregation driven by early-selection gimnázium entry from that driven by choice-based and residential mechanisms.
